questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: 7 g of stoichiometric metal oxide on reduction gives $\mathbf{5 g}$ of metal. If specific heat of metal is $0.16 \mathbf{c a l ~ g}^{-1}{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}^{-1}$. What is the exact atomic mass of metal?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Atomic mass }=\frac{6.4}{0.16}=40 \\ & \text { Equivalent weight }=\frac{5}{2} \times 8=20 \\ & \text { Equivalent weight }=\frac{\text { Atomic mass }}{n \text {-factor }} \\ & \qquad n \text {-factor }=\frac{40}{20}=2 \\ & \text { Exact atomic mass }=40\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If 4 g of metal reacts with 17.75 g of chlorine to give metal chloride. How many kilograms of metal oxide is produced from 76 kg of metal carbonate?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Eq. wt of metal }=\frac{4}{17.75} \times 35.5=8 \mathrm{~g} \\ & \mathrm{M}_2\left(\mathrm{CO}_3\right)_{\mathrm{x}} \longrightarrow \mathrm{M}_2 \mathrm{O}_{\mathrm{x}} \\ & \text { Gram equivalents of carbonate }=\text { Gram } \\ & \text { equivalents of oxide } \\ & \frac{76}{(8+30)}=\frac{\mathrm{wt}}{(8+8)} \\ & \mathrm{wt}=\frac{76 \times 16}{68}=32\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: The molarity of final mixture formed by mixing $24 \mathrm{~g}, 49 \%$ by weight $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ ( $\mathrm{d}=1.6 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{cc}$ ) and $90 \mathrm{~g}, 24.5 \%$ by weight $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4(\mathrm{~d}=1.5 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{cc})$ is $x M$. The value of 10 x is (density of resulting solution is $\left.1.52 \mathrm{~g} / \mathrm{cc}\right) ?$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $5$ ml of a gaseous hydrocarbon $\left(\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{H}_y\right)$ was exploded with 30 ml of oxygen. The resulting gas on cooling to room temperature is found to measure $25 ml of which 10 ml are absorbed by NaOH and the remainder by pyrogallol. The value of $x+y$ is?
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 5 x=10, x=2 \\ & 5\left(x+\frac{y}{4}\right)=15 \\ & y=4\end{aligned}$ Formula of hydrocarbon $=\mathrm{C}_{\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{y}}$ $x+y=2+4=6$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
A $50 \mathrm{ml} 1.92 \%(\mathrm{~W} / \mathrm{V})$ solution of a metal ion $\mathrm{M}^{\mathrm{nt}}$ (atomic weight $=60$ ) was treated with 5.332 g hydrazinehydrate ( $90 \%$ pure) and mixture was saturated with $\mathrm{CO}_2$ gas when entire metal gets precipitated as a complex $\left[\mathrm{M}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{~N}-\mathrm{NHCOO}\right)_n\right]$. The complex was filtered off and the filtrate was titrated with $\frac{\mathrm{M}}{10} \mathrm{KIO}_3$ in the presence of conc. HCl according to the following equation:
$\mathrm{N}_2 \mathrm{H}_4+\mathrm{IO}_3^{-}+2 \mathrm{H}^{+}+2 \mathrm{Cl}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{ICl}+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+\mathrm{N}_2 \uparrow$
The volume of $\frac{\mathrm{M}}{10} \mathrm{KIO}_3$ solution needed for the end point to arrive was 480 ml . Find the value of n.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{M}^{+}}=\frac{1.92}{2 \times 60} \mathrm{~mol}=0.016 \mathrm{~mol} \\ & \mathrm{n}_{\text {hydrazinehydrate }}==\frac{0.9 \times 5.332}{50} \mathrm{~mol}=0.096 \mathrm{~mol}\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{n}_{\text {eqKIO3 }}=4 \times \frac{1}{10} \mathrm{~N} \times 0.48 \mathrm{~L}=0.192 \simeq \mathrm{n}_{\text {eqhydrazine }}$ reacted with $\mathrm{KIO}_3$
$\therefore \mathrm{n}_{\text {hydrazine }}$ left after reaction with $\mathrm{M}^{\mathrm{n}+}=\frac{0.192}{4} \mathrm{~mol}=0.048 \mathrm{~mol}$
$\therefore \mathrm{n}_{\text {hydrazine }}$ reacted with $\mathrm{M}^{\mathrm{n+}}=(0.096-0.048) \mathrm{mol}=0.048 \mathrm{~mol}$
$\frac{\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{m}^{+}}}{\mathrm{n}_{\text {hydrazine }}}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{n}}=\frac{0.016}{0.048}=\frac{1}{3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A mixture of $\mathrm{O}_2$ and gas " Y " (mol. wt. 80) in the mole ratio a : b has a mean molecular weight 40 . What would be mean molecular weight, if the gases are mixed in the ratio b : a under identical conditions? (gases are non-reacting) :
questions.solution:
Let mole fraction of $\mathrm{O}_2$ is x
$40=32 \times x+80(1-x) \quad$ or $\quad x=5 / 6$
$a+b=x:(1-x)=\frac{5}{6}: \frac{1}{6}$
When ratio is changed
$M_{\text {mixture }}=32 \times \frac{1}{6}+80 \times \frac{5}{6}=72$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
